data.title: India's HDFC Life says earlier data theft had no 'material impact'
data.description:
India's HDFC Life Insurance said on Thursday that a data theft it reported in November did not result in any adverse material impact and has been resolved.

"The unauthorized exploitation was confined to a specific, identified issue," the insurer said in an exchange filing, without disclosing further details.

HDFC Life, in November, said that an unknown source shared certain customer data with the company with "mala fide intent".

data.title: BOE Could Have Three More Interest Rate Cuts in 2025, Says Investec — Market Talk
data.description: The Bank of England is likely to maintain a gradual pace of interest-rate cuts, making three more cuts in 2025 of 25 basis points each, Investec Economics' Sandra Horsfield says in a note. The impact of U.S. steel and aluminium tariffs could negatively affect U.K. businesses and push down inflation. Increased uncertainty around the implementation of the tariffs could cause businesses to hold off on investing which could also push down inflation, she says. (miriam.mukuru@wsj.com)

data.title: Acer 4Q Rev TWD66.02B >2353.TW
Acer 4Q EPS TWD0.47 >2353.TW
French Stocks Fall as Tariff Woes Persist
data.description:
The CAC 40 fell 0.4% to 7,957 on Thursday, reversing the gains from the previous session, as escalating global trade tensions continued to weigh on market sentiment.

U.S. President Donald Trump reaffirmed his intention to impose additional tariffs after the steel and aluminum duties sparked retaliatory measures from the European Union and Canada, once again warning of reciprocal tariffs set to take effect next month.

The luxury sector was under pressure, with L'Oréal dropping 1.8%, LVMH down 1.5% to its lowest point in over three months, Kering falling 0.9%, and Hermès losing 0.8%.

In contrast, the financial sector showed strength, with AXA climbing 2.1%, BNP Paribas up 1%, Crédit Agricole rising 0.6%, and Société Générale gaining 0.4%.

data.title: FIT Hon Teng Logs Higher Profit, Revenue in 2024
data.description:
FIT Hon Teng recorded a higher profit attributable to owners for 2024 of $153.7 million compared with $129.0 million a year earlier, according to a Thursday filing with the Hong Kong bourse.

Earnings per share were $0.0217, up from $0.0182 a year earlier.

Revenue saw a surge of 6.1% to $4.45 billion from $4.20 billion a year earlier.

The company's shares were down over 9% at the close of trading.

data.title: A10 Networks Plans Private Offering of $200 Million of Convertible Notes
data.description:
A10 Networks said late Wednesday it plans to launch a private offering to eligible purchasers of $200 million of convertible senior notes due 2030.

The company intends to grant the initial purchasers a 13-day option to purchase up to $25 million additional amount of notes.

Net proceeds will be used for share repurchases, potential acquisitions or investments, working capital and general corporate purposes, the company said.

data.title: Lotte Chemical to resume production at EVA plant
data.description: Lotte Chemical is set to restart its Ethylene vinyl acetate (EVA) plant in Daesan.A Polymerupdate source in South Korea informed a team member,

data.title: Crisil Keeps Unimech Aerospace's Credit Rating at A-; Outlook Revised to Positive
data.description:
Crisil affirmed its A- corporate credit rating on Unimech Aerospace and Manufacturing , according to an Indian bourse filing on Wednesday.

The rating outlook was revised to positive from stable, the filing said.

data.title: Felleskjøpet Agri SA - Successful placement of new senior unsecured bonds
data.description:
Felleskjøpet Agri SA (rated BBB-/stable by Scope Ratings) has successfullyraised NOK 1,000 million across 3 tranches with tenors of 3, 5 and 7 years. o New senior unsecured 3-year NOK 250 million bond issue with a coupon of3months Nibor + 1.45%, o New senior unsecured 5-year NOK 500 million bond issue with a couponof 3months Nibor + 1.75%, ando New senior unsecured 7-year NOK 250 million with a fixed coupon of6.05%

The bond issues will be applied for listing on Nordic ABM.

In conjunction with the potential bond issuance, Felleskjøpet Agri SA hasboughtback NOK 137 million of its outstanding senior unsecured bond FKOV14 PRO due29December 2025 (with ISIN: NO0010915374) and NOK 292 million of its outstandingsenior unsecured bond FKOV15 PRO due 17 January 2026 (with ISIN:NO0011202194).

DNB Markets and Nordea acted as Joint Bookrunners for the transaction.

For further information, please contact: Head of Treasury Felleskjøpet Agri SA, Per Olaf Stampe tel. +47 92 06 00 17

https://newsweb.oslobors.no/message/641100

data.title: Russian rouble steadies close to over six-month high vs dollar
data.description:
The Russian rouble steadied close to a more than six-month high against the dollar on Thursday as the market waited for the next development on the Ukraine war after Kyiv said it was ready to support Washington's 30-day ceasefire proposal.

The Kremlin said on Wednesday it would review details from Washington about the proposed ceasefire in Ukraine before responding, while U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio hoped a deal would be struck within days.

By 0824 GMT, the rouble was up 0.8% at 86.45 to the dollar in over-the-counter market trade. It had reached 85.1205, its strongest level since August 7, 2024, on Tuesday.

Against the Chinese yuan, the most traded foreign currency in Russia, the rouble was up 0.4% to 11.88.

The rouble is up against the dollar this year, mostly thanks to expectations of improved relations between Moscow and Washington.

The currency's strengthening could have some bearing on the central bank when it next meets to set interest rates.

Anatoly Aksakov, head of the financial committee in Russia's lower house of parliament, the State Duma, said he expected the central bank to keep rates at 21% on March 21, with high inflation preventing the central bank from being able to ease borrowing costs.

"On the other hand, we see the rouble exchange rate is strengthening and there are forecasts that it will strengthen even further," Aksakov said at a financial forum on Thursday. "Respectively, the political factor is influencing the fact that the key rate may be lowered."

Brent crude oil LCOc1, a global benchmark for Russia's main export, was up 0.4% at $71.22 a barrel.

About us

Our purpose is to make a difference by developing natural resources, improving people's lives now and for generations to come. We are trusted by our owners and partners to realise the potential of their resources. We produce minerals and metals critical to the world's energy transition from operations across the Americas, Australia and Southern Africa and we are discovering and responsibly developing our next generation of mines. We aspire to leave a positive legacy and build meaningful relationships with our partners and communities to create brighter futures together.

data.title: Melco to Pick Up Stake in Security Solutions Firm
data.description:
Melco Holdings is forming a capital and business alliance with Secure through share allocation, a security solutions business, according to a Thursday bourse filing.

Under the deal, Melco will purchase 800,000 shares in Secure at 1,819 yen each, totaling 1.46 billion yen, equating to 14.35% voting rights post-allocation.

data.title: Stall Courant and Diagonal Bio agree to a pilot test of LAMPlify®
data.description:
Diagonal Bio AB

Diagonal Bio AB (publ) ("Diagonal Bio" or "the Company") and Stall Courant, owned by Unibet founder Anders Ström and run by trotting trainer Sabine Kagebrant, have signed an agreement regarding a three-month pilot test of the LAMPlify® platform.

Stall Courant, founded in 2005 and located in Heby, Sweden, is home to 35 trotting horses and also operates a breeding program. Sabine Kagebrant joined Stall Courant in 2014 and has served as CEO since 2016. Together with the trotting horse Joviality, Kagebrant has secured victories in several major races.

"We at Stall Courant are excited to work with Diagonal Bio to evaluate LAMPlify. We want to improve the overall equine health and if LAMPlify proves to be a tool we can use for early detection of potential viral infections in our horses, we can prevent the short- and long-term effects the infections have." – Sabine Kagebrant, CEO of Stall Courant

“I am glad to announce the agreement to conduct a pilot test of LAMPlify with Stall Courant, a respected player in the market. This pilot test represents another important step in demonstrating the value of our technology in a real-world setting. We look forward to working closely with our partner and gaining valuable insights and I am confident Sabine and Stall Courant will appreciate what LAMPlify can do for them.” - Karin Wehlin, CEO of Diagonal Bio AB (publ)

This press release contains inside information that Diagonal Bio AB (publ) is required to make public pursuant to the EU Market Abuse Regulation. The information was submitted for publication on 13 March, 2025, at 09:45 CET.

For additional information about Diagonal Bio, please contact:

Karin Wehlin, CEO

Phone: +46 70 305 24 88

E-mail: kw@diagonalbio.com

Certified Adviser

Svensk Kapitalmarknadsgranskning AB

E-mail: ca@skmg.se

About Diagonal Bio

Diagonal Bio AB was founded in 2020 and is a Swedish biotechnology company with headquarters and offices in Lund. The Company develops the PANVIRAL® platform to detect and diagnose small sections of pathogen genomes, for example viruses, bacteria and fungi. The Company was founded with the goal of reducing human suffering by revolutionising global diagnostics of pathogen-borne diseases with proprietary technology. Diagonal Bio's patented technology can also be used for the less regulated market segments via LAMPlify®.

https://news.cision.com/diagonal-bio-ab/r/stall-courant-and-diagonal-bio-agree-to-a-pilot-test-of-lamplify-%2Cc4118375

Pilottest Stall Courant_250313

data.title: High-quality housing by the sea - YIT starts construction of the Asunto Oy Espoon Seaside residential apartment building project
data.description:
YIT Oyj

YIT Corporation Investor News March 13, 2025, at 10:45 a.m.

High-quality housing by the sea - YIT starts construction of the Asunto Oy Espoon Seaside residential apartment building project

YIT is starting the construction of the self-developed Asunto Oy Espoon Seaside project in Keilaniemi, Espoo. The 15-storey residential building will have a total of 83 homes. The value of the project is approximately EUR 50 million. Construction will begin in March, and the project is due to be finished by the end of 2026.

The apartments in Espoo Seaside have floor areas of 48–118.5 square metres. The residents will have access to shared workspaces, a lounge and sauna facilities with terraces with a view to the sea and the seaside promenade. Some of the apartments have unobstructed views over the sea and the first-floor homes have large yards facing the sea. The ground floors of two apartments, on the same level with the Waterfront Walkway, feature workspaces 42–49 square metres in size. A cozy children’s playground and a green roof waste room will be built in the yard. A parking hall below the yard deck features 19 parking spaces with electric vehicle charging infrastructure. Plans for Seaside include a shared water sports warehouse for stand-up paddleboards and kayaks with the neighboring building.

The apartments feature underfloor heating and cooling connected to the district heating network and preparedness for an air source heat pump. Some of the homes also have a ceiling radiator panel. Apartment-specific cold and hot water consumption monitoring helps to save water. During construction, only renewable electricity will be used on the construction site.  At least 75% of the construction waste at a construction site is being sorted for recycling.

“It’s great to be able to launch YIT’s self-developed project in the Helsinki metropolitan area, with great transport connections. High-quality homes by the sea, along Espoo’s Waterfront Walkway, in the developing Keilaniemi area have been a long-awaited novelty and have aroused a lot of interest already in the pre-marketing phase,” says SVP Harri Isoviita, YIT.

Seaside will be built in a unique residential area built between Keilaniemi’s modern office buildings. There are future plans for a sea swimming pool, waterfront saunas and a stop for scheduled boats to the archipelago in the area. Keilaniemi is served by good connections; the metro is a few minutes’ walk away and shopping centre Ainoa’s services are a four minutes’ metro ride away. The Jokeri Light Rail terminus is approximately 300 metres away.

Read more:

Espoon Seaside - apartments for sale | YIT.fi

For further information: 

YIT Corporate communications, tel. +358 44 743 7536, press@yit.fi 

Distribution: Nasdaq Helsinki, major media, www.yitgroup.com  

We build and develop sustainable living environments: functional and attractive homes, future-proof public and commercial buildings, infrastructure to support the green transition as well as industrial, production, and energy facilities to support our customers' processes. YIT's vision is to be the expert partner in developing sustainable homes, spaces, and cities - for a good life. There are approximately 4,100 professionals in our team and our revenue in 2024 was EUR 1.8 billion. YIT Corporation's shares are listed on Nasdaq Helsinki.

Read more: www.yitgroup.com and follow us on Linkedin I X I Instagram I Facebook

https://news.cision.com/yit-oyj/r/high-quality-housing-by-the-sea---yit-starts-construction-of-the-asunto-oy-espoon-seaside-residentia%2Cc4118098

data.title: Brooks Macdonald Group to Transfer Listing to London Bourse's Main Market on March 28
data.description:
Brooks Macdonald Group expects the transfer of its listing from the London stock exchange's AIM segment to the main market on March 28.

The investment management group said Thursday that its admission remains subject to regulatory approval of a prospectus and other conditions. It does not plan to raise any funds or issue new shares amid the listing transfer.

data.title: PolarCool AB (publ.) Launches New Website And Shifts Its Brand to PolarCap®
data.description:
PolarCool

The Med-Tech company PolarCool announces a strategic update to its branding and the launch of its new website, www.polarcap.com. As part of its 2025 marketing strategy, the brand will now be known as PolarCap® for global marketing and visibility, while PolarCool AB (Publ) remains the official company name. This rebrand strengthens the company's international presence and streamlines its marketing and sales efforts.

This change is a key part of PolarCool's market strategy for 2025, creating new opportunities to scale up operations, expand international reach, and increase sales.

The decision to adopt the name PolarCap® is designed to enhance market understanding and accessibility, particularly within the European market and in North America. This new brand identity reflects the company's mission to provide high-performance, scientifically proven cooling technology to improve concussion recovery and promote athlete health.

The newly developed customer website www.polarcap.com, is designed to drive customer inquiries and generate leads. The new website offers an improved user experience, streamlined navigation, with the goal of simplifying and accelerating the sales process for new customers interested in PolarCap®.

Jamie Bache, Chief Growth Officer, North America, responsible for the company's marketing strategy, comments:

-This rebrand and new website are key steps in our efforts to build a stronger presence in our current and target markets to drive sales. By adopting the name PolarCap®, we are positioning ourselves for growth in a way that resonates more clearly with our customers. The new website will provide enhanced functionality and make it easier for customers to connect with us, driving engagement and, ultimately, helping more teams adopt PolarCap® to treat concussions.

As part of the rebrand, PolarCap® will replace the previous product name, PolarCap® System. The company's official corporate name will remain PolarCool AB (publ.). For more information about PolarCap® and to explore the new website, visit www.polarcap.com

For more information

Erik Andersson -  CEO PolarCool AB (publ.)               

+46 - 738 60 57 00                                         

E-mail: erik.andersson@polarcool.se

About PolarCool AB (publ.)

PolarCool AB (publ.) is a medical device company that develops, markets, and sells products for sports medicine. The company focuses on treatment of concussive and sub-concussive brain injury with the portable cooling device PolarCap® System. PolarCool AB (publ.) is based in Lund, Sweden, and its shares are listed on Spotlight Stock Market.

https://news.cision.com/se/polarcool/r/polarcool-ab--publ--launches-new-website-and-shifts-its-brand-to-polarcap-%2Cc4117648

Press release PolarCool 2025.03.13 - PolarCool AB (publ.) Launches New Website And Shifts Its Brand to PolarCap®

data.title: Watania International Resumes Trading on Dubai Bourse
data.description:
Watania International (DFM:WATANIA) recommenced trading on the Dubai Financial Market on Thursday, according to a same-day release.

The trading suspension started Wednesday and was lifted after the investment service company published the results of its board meeting.

data.title: What is the distribution of forecasts for the US PPI?
data.description:
Why it's important?

The ranges of estimates are important in terms of market reaction because when the actual data deviates from the expectations, it creates a surprise effect. Anotherimportant input in market's reaction is the distribution of forecasts.

In fact, although we can have a range of estimates, most forecasts might be clustered on the upper bound of the range, so even if the data comes out inside the range of estimates but on the lower bound of the range, it can still create a surprise effect.

Distribution of forecasts for PPI

PPI Y/Y

  • 3.6% (5%)
  • 3.5% (5%)
  • 3.4% (14%)
  • 3.3% (36%) - consensus
  • 3.2% (23%)
  • 3.1% (14%)
  • 2.9% (5%)

PPI M/M

  • 0.4% (14%)
  • 0.3% (50%) - consensus
  • 0.2% (28%)
  • 0.1% (4%)
  • -0.1% (4%)

Core PPI Y/Y

  • 3.7% (6%)
  • 3.6% (41%)
  • 3.5% (41%) - consensus
  • 3.4% (6%)
  • 3.3% (6%)

Core PPI M/M

  • 0.4% (5%)
  • 0.3% (76%) - consensus
  • 0.2% (17%)
  • 0.0% (2%)

Wecan ignore the headline PPI as the market will focus on the Core figures. We can notice that there are wide ranges in estimates, although the bulk of the consensus is on 3.5/3.6% for the Y/Y figure and 0.3% for the M/M measure. Judging by yesterday's market reaction to the US CPI, the focus will be particularly on the details that feed into the Core PCE. Last month, the PPI saved the day as the components that feed into the PCE were all soft and lowered the estimates materially.

Here's a reminder of what Fed Chair Powell said in February: "We don't get excited about one or two good readings, and we don't get excited about one or two bad readings. We target PCE inflation because we think it's simply a better measure of inflation. And so you need to know the translation from CPI to PCE, and we get moredata on that. Tomorrow we'll get the producer price index. So, I think it's always wise. And the people who follow us closely know this, that we'll know actually what the PCE readings are late tomorrow (after the PPI release)".
